question: Will Halcyon Analytics renew its 120k contract after the botched migration, or churn by the September renewal date?

halcyon_analytics.description: The vendor company providing services under a 120k contract.

customer_organization.description: The client company whose VP Data, Marcus Webb, holds renewal authority.

person.description: A human actor in the scenario.

migration_event.description: The botched migration that caused a two-day outage.

goodwill_credit.description: A financial credit Nina Petrova can authorize to retain the customer.

renewal_decision_record.description: The formal record of the renewal decision made by Marcus Webb.

contract_active.description: Whether the 120k contract is currently active.

migration_outage_occurred.description: Records that the botched migration caused a two-day outage.

communication_status.description: Tracks whether Marcus Webb has gone silent or is responsive.

goodwill_credit_offered.description: Whether a goodwill credit has been formally offered.

renewal_decision_submitted.description: Whether Marcus Webb has submitted a renewal decision.

employs.description: Links a person to their employer organization.

holds_renewal_authority.description: Indicates which person holds sole renewal decision authority.

has_day_to_day_access.description: Links a customer-success lead to engineers they interact with.

caused_outage_for.description: Links a migration event to the affected team.

authorized_by.description: Links a goodwill credit to its authorizer.

migration_botched.description: Event where the migration fails and causes an outage.

customer_goes_silent.description: Event where Marcus Webb stops communicating.

goodwill_credit_authorized.description: Event where Nina Petrova authorizes a goodwill credit.

renewal_decision_made.description: Event where Marcus Webb makes a renewal or churn decision.

contract_renewed.description: Event where the contract is formally renewed.

contract_churned.description: Event where the contract is not renewed and ends.

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

renewal_decision_process.description: State machine for the renewal decision by Marcus Webb.

goodwill_credit_process.description: Process for Nina Petrova to authorize a goodwill credit.

renewal_decision_authority.description: Sole authority for renewal decision rests with Marcus Webb.
renewal_decision_authority.evidence: Renewal decision rests solely with Webb (from evidence summary).

goodwill_credit_limit.description: Nina Petrova can authorize up to 15k goodwill credit.

halcyon_120k_contract.description: The 120k service contract between Halcyon Analytics and the customer.

goodwill_credit_budget.description: Budget line for goodwill credits up to 15k.

communication_silence_rule.description: If Marcus Webb has not responded in 14+ days, he is considered 'gone silent'.
communication_silence_rule.rule: IF (current_time - communication_status.last_contact_date) > 14 days THEN communication_status.is_communicating = false

renewal_decision_visibility.description: Only Marcus Webb and Nina Petrova can see the renewal decision record before it is submitted.

nina_petrova.why_consequential: She can authorize goodwill credits up to 15k to influence the renewal decision.
affordances: authorize goodwill credit of up to 15k

marcus_webb.role: VP Data at the customer organization
marcus_webb.why_consequential: Sole decision-maker for contract renewal; his team suffered the outage; he has gone silent.

success_lead_tran.why_consequential: Has day-to-day access to Webb's engineers, can gather intelligence on sentiment.

unresolved_mechanisms: Whether Marcus Webb will respond to any goodwill credit offer before the September 1 deadline.
Whether the two-day outage has caused irreparable trust damage beyond financial compensation.
Whether Tran's access to engineers can yield actionable intelligence on Webb's likely decision.
assumptions: Marcus Webb is the sole decision-maker with no other stakeholders required for approval.
The goodwill credit limit of 15k is firm and cannot be exceeded without additional approval.
No other vendors or contracts are influencing Webb's decision.
The September 1 renewal date is fixed and cannot be extended.

auto_repairs: institution 'renewal_decision_authority' aggregation defaulted to single_authority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No representation of Marcus Webb's internal trust/risk calculus beyond binary communication status — silence could mean anger, negotiation, or simply being busy, but model treats it as a uniform trigger

No encoding of the actual severity of the two-day outage on the customer's operations (e.g., lost revenue, SLA penalties, reputational damage to Webb internally) — only a generic 'severity_level' string with no operational semantics
No representation of competing vendors or alternatives that Webb might be evaluating — churn is modeled as a binary decision without market context
No temporal constraints on the goodwill credit process relative to the September deadline — the model doesn't capture whether a credit offered in August has different weight than one offered in June
No representation of internal customer-side stakeholders (e.g., Webb's boss, legal team, procurement) who might override or influence his sole authority — the 'single_authority' assumption is brittle
relabeled_progress_bars: The 'renewal_decision_process' state machine is a progress bar from pending to decision, but it conflates 'customer_goes_silent' and 'goodwill_credit_authorized' as equivalent triggers to enter evaluation — these are opposite signals (silence is bad, credit is good) and should not share the same transition
The 'goodwill_credit_process' has a 'declined' state triggered by 'amount=0', but the model doesn't define how Nina decides to offer zero — it's just a magic transition with no antecedent
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes Marcus Webb's silence is a deterministic trigger for evaluation, but in reality silence could mean he's already decided to churn and is avoiding conflict — the model would then incorrectly predict a pending evaluation rather than an imminent churn

The goodwill credit max is set at 15k, but the contract is 120k — a 12.5% discount may be insufficient to offset outage damages, yet the model treats any authorized credit as a positive signal toward renewal
No encoding of whether the botched migration violated contractual SLAs — if it did, the customer may have legal grounds to terminate without penalty, making the renewal decision moot
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Marcus Webb's 'goes silent' is modeled as a clean event, but human silence is often gradual and ambiguous — the model lacks a 'communication_degradation' continuum
Nina Petrova's ability to 'authorize goodwill credit' is modeled as a binary affordance, but real humans weigh internal politics, cash flow, and precedent — none of these are captured
No encoding of emotional factors like frustration, embarrassment, or loss of face for Webb after the outage — these often drive churn more than financial credits
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'renewal_decision_submitted' as a factual event, but in reality Webb might publicly signal renewal while privately planning churn — the model has no way to distinguish stated intent from actual decision
The 'goodwill_credit_offered' fact type assumes a formal offer is made, but Nina might informally promise a credit without formalizing it — the model would miss this influence
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No representation of the technical migration platform or its reliability — if the botched migration was due to a specific tool, future migrations might also fail, affecting renewal calculus
No encoding of calendar deadlines or automated contract expiry triggers — the model relies entirely on human decision events
No representation of data integrity or security breaches during the outage — these could trigger regulatory or compliance issues that override Webb's authority
